findings.section: Description / What to do / Details / Edge cases
findings.issue: Task 16 repeatedly claims the sentinel comments in networks.ts are owned by 'Task 13' (e.g., line 24: 'via Task 13's sentinel comments', line 50: 'per Task 13', line 75: 'from Task 13', line 131: 'Task 13 inserted sentinel comments'). This is wrong. Systemic-fix §13 explicitly states 'T6 creates networks.ts with marker comments' and 'T11's deploy script does sed-style replace on the sentinel-anchored line.' Task 13 is the Security Audit task and does not touch networks.ts. An executor following T16's instructions might block on 'Task 13 must have inserted the sentinels' and be confused when T13 (Security Audit report) does not produce sentinel comments in networks.ts.
findings.fix: Replace all occurrences of 'Task 13' when referring to the networks.ts sentinel comment ownership with 'Task 6' (which creates networks.ts with sentinel comments per systemic-fix §13). Also verify line 131 in tasks/16.md: 'Task 13 inserted sentinel comments /* deploy-script:factoryAddress */ / /* deploy-script:vaultImplAddress */ on networks.ts lines' should read 'Task 6 created networks.ts with sentinel comments /* deploy-script:factoryAddress */ / /* deploy-script:vaultImplAddress */ (Task 11 performs the substitution).'

findings.section: What to do / Edge cases / Implementation hints
findings.issue: Task 16 asserts the PaymentSplitterFactory constructor takes exactly three args: '(IERC20 usdc, address platformTreasury, uint256 feeBps)' and that 'vaultImpl is deployed INSIDE the factory constructor'. However, Task 4 (the authoritative implementation task for the contracts) specifies the constructor as 'Constructor (address _usdc, address _vaultImpl, address _platformTreasury, uint16 _feeBps, address _initialOwner)' — five args, with _vaultImpl as an explicit constructor parameter, and _initialOwner for Ownable2Step. Task 16 also contradicts Task 11 which says 'Deploy PaymentSplitterFactory(usdcAddress, treasuryAddress, vaultImpl.address, 50)' (four args, with vaultImpl as an arg). Additionally, Task 16 uses 'uint256 feeBps' in its hardhat verify command while tech-spec Data Models and Task 4 both say 'uint16 feeBps'. These contradictions make the verify command ('npx hardhat verify --network arcTestnet <factoryAddress> <usdc> <platformTreasury> 50') wrong: it will fail if the actual constructor has 5 args. A wrong hardhat verify command causes verify to fail, which per Task 16's own AC is a hard blocker ('do not publish without verification').
findings.fix: Reconcile the factory constructor signature across all tasks. Task 4 is the implementation authority. If T4's constructor is (usdc, vaultImpl, platformTreasury, feeBps, initialOwner), then: (1) Task 16 'What to do' step on hardhat verify must use all 5 args: 'npx hardhat verify --network arcTestnet <factoryAddress> <usdc> <vaultImpl> <platformTreasury> 50 <ownerEoa>'; (2) Task 16 must NOT instruct 'deploy vaultImpl separately then read from factory.vaultImpl()' since if vaultImpl is a constructor arg it IS a separate deploy; (3) 'feeBps' should be typed uint16 consistently. If instead the design is that vaultImpl is deployed INSIDE the constructor (T16's claim), T4 must be updated to remove _vaultImpl from its constructor signature. The conflict must be resolved at its source (T4 and tech-spec Data Models) before T16 can be executed safely.

findings.issue: Task 16 instructs to read the vaultImpl address via 'npx hardhat console --network arcTestnet' then '(await ethers.getContractAt(...)).vaultImpl()'. However, this conflicts with the task's own constructor-args claim: if vaultImpl is created INSIDE the factory constructor with 'new PaymentVaultImpl()' (as T16 claims), the factory contract must expose a 'vaultImpl()' public getter. The tech-spec Data Models shows 'address public immutable vaultImpl' on the factory, so the getter exists in principle. But if the actual T4 implementation takes vaultImpl as a constructor arg (as T4 specifies), then the deploy script must deploy vaultImpl separately first — the current T16 instruction to 'read from factory.vaultImpl() after deploy' would still work in either case. This is a minor feasibility gap conditional on resolving the constructor-sig conflict above, not a standalone critical.
findings.fix: After resolving the constructor-sig conflict (finding above), update T16's deploy steps to be consistent with the actual T4/T11 implementation: if vaultImpl is a constructor arg, document the two-step deploy (impl first, then factory with impl address as arg); if vaultImpl is self-deployed inside the factory constructor, document the one-step deploy and confirm T4/T11 match.

findings.issue: Task 17's bash code snippets reference the variable '$REPO_ROOT' (e.g., 'cp "$REPO_ROOT/packages/middleware/src/__tests__/fixtures/x402-v1.schema.json" "$SCRATCH/..."') but $REPO_ROOT is never defined anywhere in the task. It is not set in any 'What to do' step, not in a setup block, and not mentioned in Context Files. An executor running the verification steps verbatim will encounter 'cp: /packages/middleware/...: No such file or directory' (empty variable expands to root path).
findings.fix: Add a $REPO_ROOT definition at the start of the smoke verification script block, e.g.: 'REPO_ROOT=$(git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse --show-toplevel)' or 'REPO_ROOT=$(pwd)' with a note that it must be run from the repo root. Alternatively, replace '$REPO_ROOT' with a relative path anchored to the working directory (the task already assumes the executor is in the repo root for 'npm run test:e2e').

findings.issue: Task 17 line 70 uses 'function feeBps() view returns (uint256)' in the inline viem ABI fragment, but the tech-spec Data Models and Task 4 both declare 'uint16 public feeBps'. While ABI-level decoding of uint16 and uint256 from a node -e call will not throw at runtime (both are zero-padded to 32 bytes on the wire and viem correctly decodes uint16 as uint256 if the declared return type is uint256), the ABI fragment is technically incorrect and could mislead future readers. More importantly, the comparison 'feeBps:String(await c.readContract(...))' returning '50' is still correct numerically, but the type annotation is wrong.
findings.fix: Change 'function feeBps() view returns (uint256)' to 'function feeBps() view returns (uint16)' in the inline ABI fragment to match the actual contract declaration.

findings.issue: Task 16's pre-flight provenance check uses a node -e one-liner with a heredoc-style substitution: 'process.exit(/^(9\.([5-9]|[1-9][0-9])|[1-9][0-9])/.test(\"$(npm --version)\" ) ? 0 : 1)'. This command substitution runs at shell parse time inside a double-quoted string passed to node -e. The embedded $(...) will expand in the shell correctly, but single/double quote nesting may cause shell parse errors depending on how the executor invokes this. In some shells (e.g., bash with set -e), a failing node -e exit code of 1 on the provenance-not-available path could abort the entire script if not properly guarded with '|| true' or an 'if' wrapper.
findings.fix: Wrap the provenance pre-flight in a proper if/else construct: 'if node -e "..." && [ -n "$GITHUB_ACTIONS" ]; then PROVENANCE_FLAG="--provenance"; else PROVENANCE_FLAG=""; echo "provenance: skipped"; fi' so that the check's exit code of 1 does not abort the deploy script. The current one-liner is fragile under set -e environments.

resolved_from_round_1: T17 hallucinated scripts/postdeploy/read-factory.mjs — RESOLVED: T17 now explicitly uses 'node -e' inline one-liner for on-chain reads, with an optional temporary file that is deleted immediately, and explicitly states 'no file in scripts/postdeploy/ exists'

T17 npm test:e2e missing — RESOLVED: T1 now explicitly adds 'test:e2e': 'vitest run src/__tests__/integration/' to packages/middleware/package.json scripts (confirmed in T1 lines 62-73 and systemic-fix §9); T17 correctly references this

T16 constructor call (false positive from round 1) — Round 1 flagged this as a potential false positive but it has turned out to be a REAL conflict between T4 (5-arg constructor including vaultImpl) and T16 (3-arg constructor, no vaultImpl). This is now raised as critical finding above
T17 readiness loop for HTTP smoke — RESOLVED: T17 now uses 'for i in {1..20}; do curl -sf .../health >/dev/null && break; sleep 0.2; done' instead of a fixed sleep
T17 scratch dir type:module — RESOLVED: T17 now explicitly patches package.json with type:module using 'node -e const fs=require(fs)...' immediately after npm init -y

T17 x402 schema from T9 fixture — RESOLVED: T17 explicitly references 'packages/middleware/src/__tests__/fixtures/x402-v1.schema.json' as the schema source (systemic-fix §1 and §2) and copies it to scratch before ajv validation
